heading: Applicability of Federal labor laws
label: 1209 Applicability of Federal labor laws
notes: Effective DateSection effective , pursuant to Resolution No. 71–9 of the Board of Governors. See , set out as a note preceding .July 1, 1971section 15(a) of Pub. L. 91–375section 101 of this title

children.label: a
children.text: Employee-management relations shall, to the extent not inconsistent with provisions of this title, be subject to the provisions of subchapter II of chapter 7 of title 29.

children.label: b
children.text: The provisions of chapter 11 of title 29 shall be applicable to labor organizations that have or are seeking to attain recognition under , and to such organizations, officers, agents, shop stewards, other representatives, and members to the extent to which such provisions would be applicable if the Postal Service were an employer under . In addition to the authority conferred on him under , the Secretary of Labor shall have authority, by regulation issued with the written concurrence of the Postal Service, to prescribe simplified reports for any such labor organization. The Secretary of Labor may revoke such provision for simplified forms of any such labor organization if he determines, after such investigation as he deems proper and after due notice and opportunity for a hearing, that the purposes of this chapter and of chapter 11 of title 29 would be served thereby.section 1203 of this titlesection 402 of title 29section 438 of title 29

children.label: c
children.text: Each employee of the Postal Service shall have the right, freely and without fear of penalty or reprisal, to form, join, and assist a labor organization or to refrain from any such activity, and each employee shall be protected in the exercise of this right.
